instructions.instruction: Always run syntax validation first before best practices or security checks; fix all syntax errors before proceeding to later layers

instructions.relevant_when: Starting a new GitLab CI validation workflow

instructions.instruction: Migrate deprecated `only`/`except` keywords to `rules:` syntax; treat deprecation warnings as errors that must be fixed
instructions.relevant_when: When a .gitlab-ci.yml uses only: or except: job keywords

instructions.instruction: Validate all local include targets referenced by the pipeline, not only the main .gitlab-ci.yml file
instructions.relevant_when: When a .gitlab-ci.yml uses include:local or include:file directives

instructions.instruction: Fix all critical and high-severity security issues immediately; do not defer security findings
instructions.relevant_when: When security scan reports critical or high-severity issues

instructions.instruction: Never run --strict as the first validation step on an unfamiliar pipeline; establish a baseline first by running without --strict
instructions.relevant_when: When considering whether to enable strict mode

instructions.instruction: Use `needs:` for DAG-based parallelism instead of sequential stage ordering when jobs are independent
instructions.relevant_when: When best practices check identifies sequential job execution that could be parallelized

instructions.instruction: Pin Docker image versions; never accept unpinned images (e.g., node:latest) in job definitions
instructions.relevant_when: When a job definition uses a Docker image without a specific version tag

instructions.instruction: Report security findings with severity level, job name, line reference, and a specific remediation step
Use `rules:` syntax for conditional job execution; always check that stage references in jobs match declared stages
instructions.relevant_when: When validating job stage references and conditional execution logic
